---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 077bc05b2edfaf1209736476a20bd4afd89634ee
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcj-entry-vs-root-selector, kind: judged, text: "dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 explicitly pins as the scope field's semantics ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). dc-3 discloses its reasoning inline (--root is documented as mermaid-render-only, --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space), but this is a reinterpretation of the parent decision's named mechanism with no declared supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-6. If the shared-grammar claim fails at build time (a --root value --entry rejects), the scope field's pinned meaning and the regeneration mechanism silently diverge; the edge should be declared rather than carried as an inline disclosure. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-dcj-ambiguity-demotes-full-coverage, kind: judged, text: "dc-2 makes a shortName collision between two truth nodes demote the WHOLE artifact to partial coverage, but parent dc-3 defines the coverage tiers by the flowchart's vocabulary alone: full = 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial = 'flowchart beyond it'. Under dc-2, a pristine generator-produced flowchart entirely within the vocabulary can still be classified partial because of a property of the truth graph (name collisions under this package's deliberately-narrower shortName), not of the flowchart. This extends parent dc-3's partial trigger from a vocabulary test to a classification-confidence test without a declared edge — and since flowmap's own renderer disambiguates collisions while dc-2's shortName does not, generator-emitted disambiguated ids may systematically fail to match, recreating the same 'pristine diagram cannot reach full' gap dc-1 was corrected to avoid for comments/classDefs. (confidence 0.55)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-dcj-witness-labels-outside-ratified-batch, kind: judged, text: "dc-4 introduces new disclosed vocabulary on the verification surface — 'candidate-witness' and 'witness-unresolved' labels attached to kept-but-gone elements — but parent dc-7 ratifies the schema surface as one closed 02 amendment batch (diagram status vocabulary, derived_from plus base-digest, scope field, illustrative marker) specifically because co-2 needs a stable artifact contract before the extractor is built. If the witness labels are part of any persisted or schema-decoded artifact rather than pure render-time presentation, they are contract surface added outside the ratified batch with no declared edge against dc-7. (confidence 0.35)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8, spec/verification-extractor#dc-1, spec/verification-extractor#dc-2, spec/verification-extractor#dc-3, spec/verification-extractor#dc-4, spec/verification-extractor#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:a9c9a3566e77a24e6cb4df47ccd2bf094515e6d295f2de6d296c90875ae315a7
integrity: sha256:b623e1b5623e2bb420810ed8f82e6f82be3e48e90c2f0790d9855a32cd3bd7ac
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dcj-entry-vs-root-selector\",\"text\":\"dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 explicitly pins as the scope field's semantics ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). dc-3 discloses its reasoning inline (--root is documented as mermaid-render-only, --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space), but this is a reinterpretation of the parent decision's named mechanism with no declared supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-6. If the shared-grammar claim fails at build time (a --root value --entry rejects), the scope field's pinned meaning and the regeneration mechanism silently diverge; the edge should be declared rather than carried as an inline disclosure.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"dcj-ambiguity-demotes-full-coverage\",\"text\":\"dc-2 makes a shortName collision between two truth nodes demote the WHOLE artifact to partial coverage, but parent dc-3 defines the coverage tiers by the flowchart's vocabulary alone: full = 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial = 'flowchart beyond it'. Under dc-2, a pristine generator-produced flowchart entirely within the vocabulary can still be classified partial because of a property of the truth graph (name collisions under this package's deliberately-narrower shortName), not of the flowchart. This extends parent dc-3's partial trigger from a vocabulary test to a classification-confidence test without a declared edge — and since flowmap's own renderer disambiguates collisions while dc-2's shortName does not, generator-emitted disambiguated ids may systematically fail to match, recreating the same 'pristine diagram cannot reach full' gap dc-1 was corrected to avoid for comments/classDefs.\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"dcj-witness-labels-outside-ratified-batch\",\"text\":\"dc-4 introduces new disclosed vocabulary on the verification surface — 'candidate-witness' and 'witness-unresolved' labels attached to kept-but-gone elements — but parent dc-7 ratifies the schema surface as one closed 02 amendment batch (diagram status vocabulary, derived_from plus base-digest, scope field, illustrative marker) specifically because co-2 needs a stable artifact contract before the extractor is built. If the witness labels are part of any persisted or schema-decoded artifact rather than pure render-time presentation, they are contract surface added outside the ratified batch with no declared edge against dc-7.\",\"confidence\":0.35,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/verification-extractor@077bc05b2edfaf1209736476a20bd4afd89634ee], digest: sha256:a9c9a3566e77a24e6cb4df47ccd2bf094515e6d295f2de6d296c90875ae315a7, integrity: sha256:b623e1b5623e2bb420810ed8f82e6f82be3e48e90c2f0790d9855a32cd3bd7ac }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcj-entry-vs-root-selector** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 explicitly pins as the scope field's semantics ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). dc-3 discloses its reasoning inline (--root is documented as mermaid-render-only, --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space), but this is a reinterpretation of the parent decision's named mechanism with no declared supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-6. If the shared-grammar claim fails at build time (a --root value --entry rejects), the scope field's pinned meaning and the regeneration mechanism silently diverge; the edge should be declared rather than carried as an inline disclosure. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-dcj-ambiguity-demotes-full-coverage** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: dc-2 makes a shortName collision between two truth nodes demote the WHOLE artifact to partial coverage, but parent dc-3 defines the coverage tiers by the flowchart's vocabulary alone: full = 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial = 'flowchart beyond it'. Under dc-2, a pristine generator-produced flowchart entirely within the vocabulary can still be classified partial because of a property of the truth graph (name collisions under this package's deliberately-narrower shortName), not of the flowchart. This extends parent dc-3's partial trigger from a vocabulary test to a classification-confidence test without a declared edge — and since flowmap's own renderer disambiguates collisions while dc-2's shortName does not, generator-emitted disambiguated ids may systematically fail to match, recreating the same 'pristine diagram cannot reach full' gap dc-1 was corrected to avoid for comments/classDefs. (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-dcj-witness-labels-outside-ratified-batch** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: dc-4 introduces new disclosed vocabulary on the verification surface — 'candidate-witness' and 'witness-unresolved' labels attached to kept-but-gone elements — but parent dc-7 ratifies the schema surface as one closed 02 amendment batch (diagram status vocabulary, derived_from plus base-digest, scope field, illustrative marker) specifically because co-2 needs a stable artifact contract before the extractor is built. If the witness labels are part of any persisted or schema-decoded artifact rather than pure render-time presentation, they are contract surface added outside the ratified batch with no declared edge against dc-7. (confidence 0.35)
